task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `VideoEditorPage` to destructure `settings` from `useToolSettings` and reordered the auto-select effect so it now: exits if `timelineId` already exists, seeds immediately from `settings?.lastTimelineId` without waiting on `timelines.isLoading`, falls back to `timelines.data?.[0]` when the list has loaded cleanly, and otherwise preserves the existing auto-create branch. Verified in source that `settings?.lastTimelineId` is included in the dependency array, then ran `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` successfully.

Updated `VideoEditorPage` so the existing persist effect now exits when `timelines.data` has loaded and does not contain the active `timelineId`, which prevents stale IDs from being written back into tool settings. Added a new validation effect immediately after the persist effect that clears both `lastTimelineId` and the URL search param when a loaded timelines list does not contain the active ID. Verified the effect order in source and ran `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` successfully.

Replaced the non-error loading placeholder in `VideoEditorPage` with `return null;` while keeping the existing `timelines.error` card path unchanged. Re-grepped the file after the edit to confirm `Card`, `CardContent`, `CardHeader`, `CardTitle`, `CardDescription`, and `Skeleton` are still used elsewhere, so no import removals were necessary. Verified the file still type-checks with `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit`.

Updated `ToolsPaneComponent` to subscribe to `useToolSettings(videoEditorSettings.id, { projectId, enabled })` using the selected project ID so video-editor settings stay prefetched during the session. Adjusted `handleNavigate` so navigation to `/tools/video-editor` appends `?timeline=<lastTimelineId>` when cached project settings already contain a last-opened timeline; other tool paths still navigate unchanged. Verified the new imports do not reference `ToolsPane` and ran `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` successfully.

Updated `useGlobalPaneShortcuts` so the Opt+Shift+W path peeks the React Query cache with `queryClient.getQueryData(queryKeys.settings.tool(videoEditorSettings.id, selectedProjectId ?? undefined, undefined))` and unwraps the cached settings via `extractSettingsFromCache` without triggering a fetch. When the cached project settings contain `lastTimelineId`, the shortcut now navigates to `/tools/video-editor?timeline=<id>`; otherwise it preserves the existing bare `/tools/video-editor` navigation. Verified the hook still type-checks with `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit`.

Ran the requested verification sequence on the modified workspace. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` was blocked by the repo's npm-only packageManager setting, and the equivalent `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` passed. Focused vitest passed for `src/tools/video-editor/registration.test.ts`; the `src/shared/components/ToolsPane` target had no test files and exited cleanly with `--passWithNoTests`. The full `npm test` suite completed against the current workspace but failed in many unrelated pre-existing areas outside the touched files, including unresolved imports and older assertion mismatches in unrelated suites. Wrote a throwaway `VideoEditorPage` repro test covering the cached-valid-id and stale-id recovery scenarios, ran it successfully, and deleted it afterward.
commands_run: npm exec vitest -- run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/components/ToolsPane --passWithNoTests

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the rewritten auto-select effect now sets `?timeline=<settings.lastTimelineId>` immediately at `VideoEditorPage.tsx:215-217` without waiting on `timelines.isLoading`, keeps the `timelines.data?.[0]` fallback at `:220-224`, preserves the auto-create branch below that, and includes `settings?.lastTimelineId` in the dependency array at `:244`.

Confirmed `VideoEditorPage.tsx` now clears both the URL param and `lastTimelineId` when loaded `timelines.data` does not contain the active `timelineId`, the persist effect early-returns on that same stale-id condition so it cannot re-write the stale value, and the validation effect is declared immediately after the persist effect.

Confirmed the loading placeholder branch in `VideoEditorPage.tsx` now returns `null`, the `timelines.error` card branch remains intact, and the post-edit grep showed the card and skeleton imports are still used elsewhere in the file so there are no dangling imports to remove.

Confirmed `ToolsPane.tsx` now subscribes to `useToolSettings` with `projectId: selectedProjectId ?? undefined` and `enabled: Boolean(selectedProjectId)`, and `handleNavigate` rewrites `/tools/video-editor` to include `?timeline=<lastTimelineId>` when cached settings are present. The added imports do not point back at `ToolsPane` based on the inspected import graph and the clean typecheck.

Confirmed `useGlobalPaneShortcuts.ts` now uses `queryClient.getQueryData` with the same `queryKeys.settings.tool(videoEditorSettings.id, selectedProjectId ?? undefined, undefined)` key shape as `useToolSettings`, appends `?timeline=<id>` only when cached settings expose `lastTimelineId`, and otherwise falls back to `/tools/video-editor`.

Recorded the full verification sweep: `pnpm tsc --noEmit` was blocked by the repo's npm-only packageManager setting, `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` passed, focused vitest passed for `src/tools/video-editor/registration.test.ts`, `src/shared/components/ToolsPane` had no dedicated tests, the full suite completed but failed in many unrelated existing areas, and the throwaway `VideoEditorPage` repro passed both the valid cached-id and deleted cached-id scenarios before the file was deleted.
